script.speaker: Interviewer
script.text: Hello, thank you for coming in today. Can you tell me a little bit about yourself?

script.speaker: Candidate
script.text: My name is Sarah, and I have 3 years of experience in marketing. I'm excited about the opportunity to work at Johnson & Co.

script.speaker: Interviewer
script.text: Great, we're looking for someone with strong communication skills. Can you give me an example of a successful campaign you've led?

script.speaker: Candidate
script.text: In my previous role at ABC Corporation, I led a campaign that increased sales by 25% within 6 months. We spent a budget of 10,000 dollars and reached over 1 million people.

script.speaker: Interviewer
script.text: That's impressive. We're looking for someone to start as soon as possible, ideally within the next 2 weeks.

script.speaker: Customer
script.text: I'd like to book a table for 4 people at 7:30 pm on Friday, please.

script.speaker: Restaurant Staff
script.text: Certainly, we have a table available at that time. It will be 20 dollars per person for the buffet.

script.speaker: Customer
script.text: That sounds great. Can I also book a cake for my friend's birthday? It's her 30th birthday.

script.speaker: Restaurant Staff
script.text: We can arrange a cake for 50 dollars. And we'll also give you a 10% discount on the total bill if you pay by cash.

script.speaker: Customer
script.text: That's wonderful, thank you. Can you confirm my booking?

script.speaker: Restaurant Staff
script.text: Yes, your booking is confirmed. We'll see you on Friday at 7:30 pm. The address is 123 Main Street, and our phone number is 555-1234.

intermediate.title: The History of Coffee
intermediate.content: Coffee has a rich and diverse history that spans over 1,000 years. The earliest credible evidence of coffee drinking comes from the 15th century, in the Sufi shrines of Yemen. From there, coffee spread throughout the Arabian Peninsula, and by the 16th century, it had reached the rest of the Middle East, as well as Europe. The first coffeehouses were established in the Middle East, where they became important centers of social and intellectual activity. In Europe, coffeehouses became popular gathering places for artists, writers, and intellectuals, and were often associated with revolutionary and liberal ideas. The first coffeehouse in Europe was established in Venice in 1683, and soon coffeehouses were springing up all over the continent. As trade and colonization expanded, coffee became a major commodity, and its production and trade had a significant impact on the economies of many countries. The coffee plant was first cultivated in the Americas in the 18th century, and by the 19th century, Brazil had become the world's largest producer of coffee. Today, coffee is grown in over 70 countries, and is one of the most widely traded commodities in the world. The history of coffee is also closely tied to the history of slavery, as many coffee plantations in the Americas were worked by enslaved Africans. The abolition of slavery in the 19th century had a significant impact on the coffee industry, as many plantations were forced to adapt to new labor systems. The 20th century saw the rise of instant coffee, as well as the development of new coffee-making technologies, such as the espresso machine. Today, coffee is a major part of many cultures around the world, and is enjoyed in a wide variety of ways. From the traditional Turkish coffee to the modern-day latte, coffee has become an integral part of many people's daily lives. The history of coffee is a complex and fascinating story, and one that continues to evolve to this day. As the coffee industry continues to grow and change, it is likely that the history of coffee will remain an important and fascinating topic for many years to come.

questions.question: Where is the earliest credible evidence of coffee drinking from?
questions.explanation: The passage states that the earliest credible evidence of coffee drinking comes from the 15th century, in the Sufi shrines of Yemen.

questions.question: What were the first coffeehouses established in?
questions.explanation: The passage states that the first coffeehouses were established in the Middle East, where they became important centers of social and intellectual activity.

questions.question: What was the first coffeehouse in Europe established in?
questions.explanation: The passage states that the first coffeehouse in Europe was established in Venice in 1683.

questions.question: What was the impact of the abolition of slavery on the coffee industry?
options: It led to a decline in coffee production
It forced many plantations to adapt to new labor systems
It led to an increase in coffee consumption
questions.answer: It forced many plantations to adapt to new labor systems
questions.explanation: The passage states that the abolition of slavery in the 19th century had a significant impact on the coffee industry, as many plantations were forced to adapt to new labor systems.

questions.question: What is one of the most widely traded commodities in the world?
questions.explanation: The passage states that coffee is one of the most widely traded commodities in the world.

questions.question: What is the history of coffee closely tied to?
questions.explanation: The passage states that the history of coffee is closely tied to the history of slavery, as many coffee plantations in the Americas were worked by enslaved Africans, and is also closely tied to the history of trade and colonization, as the production and trade of coffee had a significant impact on the economies of many countries.
